mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth and consumer credit expansion jointly drive corporate earnings expectations. Sustained positive growth in chained 2017 dollars and rising consumer credit balances reduce equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals impending recessionary pressure, forcing equity valuations to discount future cash flows at higher risk premiums. When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a single month, S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days turn negative.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of months where UNRATE jumps >0.5pp, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates compress when nominal policy rates fall while inflation expectations remain anchored. A simultaneous decline in the federal funds rate and stable CPIAUCSL readings indicate accommodative monetary conditions that lower discount rates and boost equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not significantly higher when FEDFUNDS declines and CPIAUCSL remains flat compared to periods of rising rates or falling inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth directly reflects corporate capacity utilization and margin expansion. During post-recession recovery phases, rising INDPRO readings signal pent-up demand absorption and operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO growth during recovery quarters, or negative correlation emerges across multiple historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth serves as a leading indicator of consumer spending and corporate revenue stability. Consistent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reduce earnings uncertainty, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in payroll growth months are statistically indistinguishable from flat employment months.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts act as a high-frequency barometer for business and consumer confidence. When monthly housing starts fall below 1.0 million units, it signals a credit or demand shock that suppresses equity valuations over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions turn negative.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of months where HOUST drops below 1.0 million units, or the threshold shows no incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A dual shock of rising policy rates and deteriorating labor conditions forces a repricing of equity risk. When the federal funds rate increases while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises, the resulting stagflationary pressure compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly lower when FEDFUNDS rises and UNRATE increases compared to periods where only one variable moves adversely.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer credit expansion accelerates ahead of seasonal retail demand spikes. During pre-holiday quarters, rising RSAFS balances combined with positive GDPC1 growth indicate strong consumer liquidity, driving short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns do not exhibit positive skew when RSAFS and GDPC1 both rise in Q4, or negative returns dominate despite credit growth across multiple years.
